testcase.name: test_async_get_forecast_by_coordinates
test_async_get_forecast_by_place_name
test_async_get_weather_by_coordinates
test_async_get_weather_by_place
test_get_forecast_by_coordinates
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_get_forecast_by_coordinates> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4377286160'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_coordinate_forecast_response) def test_get_forecast_by_coordinates(self, mock_get): > forecast = fmi_weather_client.forecast_by_coordinates(29.742731, 67.583988) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test/test_init.py:49: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fmi_weather_client/__init__.py:97: in forecast_by_coordinates response = http.request_forecast_by_coordinates(lat, lon, timestep_hours) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:54: in request_forecast_by_coordinates return _send_request(params) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:138: in _send_request _handle_errors(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
response = <Response [400 Bad Request]>
def _handle_errors(response: httpx.Response): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status_code < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.text) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status_code, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:149: ClientError

testcase.name: test_get_forecast_by_place_name
test_get_weather_by_coordinates
test_get_weather_by_place
test_nil_forecast_response
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_nil_forecast_response> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4377404368'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_nan_response) def test_nil_forecast_response(self, mock_get): > forecast_coord = fmi_weather_client.forecast_by_coordinates(25.46816, 65.01236, 24) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
test/test_init.py:68: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fmi_weather_client/__init__.py:97: in forecast_by_coordinates response = http.request_forecast_by_coordinates(lat, lon, timestep_hours) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:54: in request_forecast_by_coordinates return _send_request(params) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:138: in _send_request _handle_errors(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
response = <Response [400 Bad Request]>
def _handle_errors(response: httpx.Response): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status_code < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.text) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status_code, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:149: ClientError

testcase.name: test_nil_weather_response
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_nil_weather_response> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4377136784'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_nan_response) def test_nil_weather_response(self, mock_get): > weather_coord = fmi_weather_client.weather_by_coordinates(25.46816, 65.01236) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^

response = <Response [400 Bad Request]>
def _handle_errors(response: httpx.Response): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status_code < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.text) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status_code, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:149: ClientError

testcase.name: test_server_error_response
testcase.failure:
self = <test.test_init.FMIWeatherTest testMethod=test_server_error_response> mock_get = <MagicMock name='get' id='4377320208'>
@mock.patch('requests.get', side_effect=test_data.mock_server_error_response) def test_server_error_response(self, mock_get): with self.assertRaises(ServerError): > fmi_weather_client.weather_by_coordinates(27.31317, 63.14343)
test/test_init.py:94: _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ fmi_weather_client/__init__.py:18: in weather_by_coordinates response = http.request_weather_by_coordinates(lat, lon) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:29: in request_weather_by_coordinates return _send_request(params) ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ fmi_weather_client/http.py:138: in _send_request _handle_errors(response) _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _ _
def _handle_errors(response: httpx.Response): """Handle error responses from FMI service""" if 400 <= response.status_code < 500: data = xmltodict.parse(response.text) try: error_message = data['ExceptionReport']['Exception']['ExceptionText'][0] > raise ClientError(response.status_code, error_message) E fmi_weather_client.errors.ClientError
fmi_weather_client/http.py:149: ClientError
